hypothesis: Modifying the DPO reward to require BCC–B2 coexistence with ≤10% other phases and nonzero B2 fraction at all temperatures ≥1473 K, and weighting lattice mismatch only over that range, will cause a temperature-conditioned LLaMA‑3.1‑8B to generate ≥25% of candidates whose coexistence window lies entirely above 1473 K while maintaining coverage recall ≥0.95 and Unique pairs @100 ≥0.90, whereas the unconditioned DPO model will yield ≤5% such candidates.
